text: {"title":"Gender-context lifestyle typicality","group_name":"gender_lifestyle_category_typicality","family":"peer_context_categorical_typicality","summary":"Represent whether a student's reported lifestyle categories are common or unusual among students with the same reported gender, treating demographic moderation of health behaviors as a compact context signal.","depends_on":[],"strategy":"Use the original categorical lifestyle fields diet_type, stress_level, sleep_quality, physical_activity_level, and smoking_alcohol, with gender as the peer context. Convert missing gender and missing lifestyle values to explicit tokens. Fit unsupervised smoothed frequency tables on training feature rows only, excluding the target: for each lifestyle field compute p(value | gender) and p(value) with additive smoothing alpha=1 over all observed levels plus the missing token. For each row derive per-field conditional surprise -log(p(value | gender)), overall surprise -log(p(value)), and gender log-lift log(p(value | gender)) - log(p(value)). Return one coherent group of aggregates across the five lifestyle fields: mean conditional surprise, maximum conditional surprise, sum conditional surprise, count of fields where p(value | gender) is below 0.20, mean gender log-lift, sum absolute gender log-lift, and summed conditional log probability. For unseen values or gender contexts, fall back to the smoothed overall probability for that field; missing values are handled as their own category rather than imputed.","expected_signal":"Published student-health and physical-activity literature emphasizes that lifestyle patterns and their health associations can differ by gender, so same-gender categorical typicality may reveal risk-relevant behavior context that raw category labels alone do not express, especially for minority health_condition classes.","risk":"This may be redundant with raw categorical variables, broader co-occurrence-density features, and existing gender-relative numeric context; if the synthetic data generation makes lifestyle categories nearly independent of gender, the features will add little signal and could introduce small distribution-instability noise."}
